question: A task could be solved by a cheap local model in one attempt, or a large cloud model with certainty. The cost difference is 50x. When is it correct to spend the 50x anyway?
An agent must decide whether to re-read an entire large file (expensive) or trust its cached memory of the file's contents from three turns ago (risky if changed). What should determine this choice?
A verification step doubles the cost of every agent action but catches errors 10% of the time. Under what conditions does this verification step pay for itself?
An agent can batch 10 similar file edits into one large tool call, or make 10 separate calls. What are the cost and risk tradeoffs of each approach?
Given a fixed budget of tool calls for a task, is it better to spend early calls on exploration (reading broadly) or go straight for the most likely fix? Explain the reasoning.
